question: Contributor is assigned at resource group scope. A deny assignment blocks the same resources. What applies?
answer: Deny assignment overrides the role assignment
explanation: In Azure RBAC, deny assignments override role assignments. This ensures explicit blocking of access takes precedence regardless of scope. Role assignments are additive, but denials cannot be bypassed.

options.text: Deny assignment overrides the role assignment
Role assignment overrides the deny assignment
Both assignments are additive without override
The most recent assignment timestamp takes priority

question: Azure Policy is assigned at the management group scope. Which scopes does it apply to?
answer: All child management groups and subscriptions
explanation: Azure Policy at the management group scope applies to all child management groups, subscriptions, and resource groups. Inheritance is hierarchical and mandatory. Child management groups cannot block inherited policies.

options.text: All child management groups and subscriptions
Only subscriptions under direct management groups
Management group and its direct children only
